analysis: Tested an ensemble of 5 independent runs of the successful Geometric Affinity Pre-training Curriculum. The variance across the 5 independent pre-train/fine-tune cycles was extremely high: validation AUCs ranged from 0.4516 to 0.5978 (Fold 4). Averaging the predictions of all 5 folds yielded a final ensemble AUC of 0.5327. This reveals that the strong performance of Iteration 2 (0.5967) was dependent on a specific randomized generation of the 4,000 synthetic shapes and network initialization. Simply ensembling all models dilutes the performance because the collapsed/poorly-initialized models pull down the score. Model selection or filtering is needed before ensembling.
